text: basic example shows solve common problem:
code: library(MarConsNetData)  bioregion <- data_bioregion() #> Warning in CPL_crs_from_input(x): GDAL Message 1: +init=epsg:XXXX syntax is #> deprecated. It might return a CRS with a non-EPSG compliant axis order.

what: A Package for Locating and Transforming MPA Data — MarConsNetData-package
title: A Package for Locating and Transforming MPA Data — MarConsNetData-package
text: MarConsNetData package locate transform real data collected variety different Marine Protected Areas (MPAs).

what: Get Canadian Protected and Conserved Areas Database — data_CPCAD_areas
title: Get Canadian Protected and Conserved Areas Database — data_CPCAD_areas
text: function gets polygons Canadian protected conserved areas specified bio region. information visit https://www.canada.ca/en/environment-climate-change/services/ national-wildlife-areas/protected-conserved-areas-database.html

title: Get Canadian Protected and Conserved Areas Database — data_CPCAD_areas
Get Canadian Protected and Conserved Areas Database — data_CPCAD_areas
text: bioregion sf data.frame bio region. can use MarConsNetData::get_bioregion() download bio region choice. zones logical indicates zones returned (TRUE) , default, unioned (FALSE). zones FALSE, sf data.frame grouped NAME_E geoms unioned resulting complete loss columns.

title: Get Canadian Protected and Conserved Areas Database — data_CPCAD_areas
text: "sf" "dataframe" object

title: Get Canadian Protected and Conserved Areas Database — data_CPCAD_areas
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ bioregion <- data_bioregion() MPAs <- data_CPCAD_areas(bioregion,  zones = FALSE) } # }

Get climate data — data_PAVICS
text: Get data PAVICS THREDDS catalog

title: Get climate data — data_PAVICS
code: data_PAVICS(   OPENDAP_url =     "https://pavics.ouranos.ca/twitcher/ows/proxy/thredds/dodsC/datasets/simulations/RCM-CMIP6/CORDEX/NAM-12/day/NAM-12_NorESM2-MM_historical_r1i1p1f1_OURANOS_CRCM5_v1-r1_day_19500101-20141231.ncml",   first_date = "1950-01-01",   end_date = "1951-01-1",   variable = "tas",   geom = data_planning_areas(),   maskGeom = TRUE )

title: Get climate data — data_PAVICS
text: OPENDAP_url character string OPENDAP url THREDDS catalog. Defaults RCM-CMIP6 daily simulation first_date character string first date included data subset. Defaults "1950-01-01" end_date character string last date included data subset. Defaults "1950-01-01" variable character string variable query data. Defaults "tas" Near-Surface Air Temperature. See OPENDAP url options geom sf data.frame used limit spatial extent data subset (via bounding box). Defaults Scotian Shelf Bay Fundy planning area maskGeom logical defaults TRUE turn values outside geom NA instead returning values downloaded .

title: Get climate data — data_PAVICS
text: matrix

title: Get climate data — data_PAVICS
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ require(MarConsNetData) require(dplyr) # get the Scotian Shelf polygon ssbof <- data_planning_areas()  # get CMIP6 data dor Near-Surface Air Temperature (tas) climate <- data_PAVICS(OPENDAP_url = "https://pavics.ouranos.ca/twitcher/ows/proxy/thredds/dodsC/datasets/simulations/RCM-CMIP6/CORDEX/NAM-12/day/NAM-12_NorESM2-MM_historical_r1i1p1f1_OURANOS_CRCM5_v1-r1_day_19500101-20141231.ncml",                        first_date = "1950-01-01",                        end_date = "1950-03-1",                        variable = "tas",                        geom = ssbof,                        maskGeom = TRUE)  # plot a single time point climate |>   slice("time",1) |>   plot()  # calculate and plot monthly means monthlyClimate <- aggregate(climate,                             by = "1 month",                             FUN = mean,                             na.rm = TRUE)  plot(monthlyClimate)  } # }

what: Build a cleaned eDNA sampling dataset from the raw eDNA-for-MPAs directory — data_eDNA
title: Build a cleaned eDNA sampling dataset from the raw eDNA-for-MPAs directory — data_eDNA
text: function recursively scans ../eDNA--MPAs/data/ directory locate CSV files containing "GOTeDNA" file name path. Files grouped cruise mission, metadata data files paired, naming inconsistencies corrected, unified table sample-level information produced.

title: Build a cleaned eDNA sampling dataset from the raw eDNA-for-MPAs directory — data_eDNA
Build a cleaned eDNA sampling dataset from the raw eDNA-for-MPAs directory — data_eDNA
text: data frame containing: ID Sample identifier date Sampling date (class Date) latitude Sample latitude longitude Sample longitude (corrected negative values) species_richness Count unique species detected sample method eDNA sampling method inferred folder structure location Sampling location identifier year Extracted sampling year

title: Build a cleaned eDNA sampling dataset from the raw eDNA-for-MPAs directory — data_eDNA
text: function performs several formatting corrections, including: resolving inconsistent materialSampleID naming (e.g., X, _, -) handling metadata files different column name conventions resolving location name inconsistencies (e.g., ESI, SAB) extracting sample dates, coordinates, richness, method type correcting positive longitudes flipping sign final result single cleaned data frame one row per sample. function assumes following: directory ../eDNA--MPAs/data/ exists contains cruise folders. Metadata files contain either materialSampleID, SampleID, eDNA_Tube. Species columns data files may named Species, species, V6. Latitude/longitude may stored decimalLatitude/decimalLongitude Lat/Long. function prints progress messages (, j, k, l) may enter browser() unexpected inconsistent cases.

title: Build a cleaned eDNA sampling dataset from the raw eDNA-for-MPAs directory — data_eDNA
code:
if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ df <- data_eDNA() # Quick map preview leaflet::leaflet(df) %>% leaflet::addTiles() %>% leaflet::addCircleMarkers( lat = ~latitude, lng = ~longitude, popup = paste("Lat:", df$latitude, "
Lon:", df$longitude) ) } # }
